item.text: Secondary Diagnosis: Provider Note: If L98.7 or M79.3 is the primary diagnosis, you must include one of the following as a secondary diagnosis: E65, L30.4, R26.2, or Z74.09 if required by the LCD.

valueCoding.display: R26.2 – Difficulty in walking, not elsewhere classified

item.text: Pannus (panniculus adiposus): Apron of excess skin and subcutaneous fat of anterior abdominal wall with excessive hanging/drooping of pannus extends below symphysis pubis.

valueCoding.display: Grade 1 – The panniculus reaches the pubic hair but not the genitals
Grade 2 – The panniculus lies over the genitals down to the thigh crease
Grade 3 – The panniculus reaches down to the upper thigh
Grade 4 – The panniculus hangs down to the mid-thigh level
Grade 5 – The panniculus reaches the knees

item.text: Inability to walk normally due to pannus size.
Preoperative photographs to support justification if required by LCD
If surgery is medically necessary due to significant weight loss, stable weight requirement must be met

valueCoding.display: Stable weight for six months after significant weight loss to treat morbid obesity

Weight loss is result of bariatric surgery that occurred at least 18 months ago, and weight has been stable for at least six months, and infection and inflammation has continued for the most recent three months
